description: Evaluates compliance management service maturity—compliance platform and scanning, process and standards alignment (including DORA and EU AI Act), metrics and teams, endpoint posture policy, asset hardening, SOC transport of compliance changes, and AI-era compliance governance.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: A compliance management service is defined in the enterprise and reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy.
evaluation_focus: Compliance management service defined in the enterprise
Quarterly service efficacy review

capabilities.name: Compliance management platform and scanning
capabilities.description: Compliance management software monitors compliance enterprise-wide with automated scanning, configuration review, remediation integration, and attribute-based decision support.
evaluation_focus: Compliance management software for enterprise-wide monitoring
Scope across enterprise and subsidiaries
Automated compliance scanning across all assets and locations
Software configuration reviewed at least quarterly to align with business
Scan results integrated with remediation and decision points

capabilities.name: Compliance management process
capabilities.description: An established, documented compliance management process is implemented enterprise-wide or in defined scope, incorporates automation, and is reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Established documented compliance management process
Process implemented enterprise-wide or scoped
Automation incorporated into the process
Quarterly process review

capabilities.name: Standards and regulations alignment
capabilities.description: Relevant standards and regulations—including DORA, EU AI Act, and sector rules—are officially documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Official documentation of relevant standards and regulations
DORA and financial-sector ICT compliance obligations mapped where applicable
EU AI Act and AI system compliance obligations mapped where applicable
Quarterly review of compliance requirements

capabilities.name: Compliance metrics
capabilities.description: Compliance KPIs and KRIs—including control coverage, scan pass rate, mean time to remediate findings, and regulatory attestation status—are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Officially designed and documented compliance metrics
Control coverage and scan pass rate metrics
Mean time to remediate compliance findings
Quarterly review of compliance metrics

capabilities.name: Compliance teams and specialists
capabilities.description: Compliance management teams with policy and compliance specialists govern the overall compliance management service.
evaluation_focus: Compliance management teams manage the overall service
Policy and compliance specialists on compliance teams

capabilities.name: Endpoint compliance (posture) policy
capabilities.description: Endpoint compliance policy is formalized, scoped enterprise-wide or to defined devices and locations, and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Formal documented endpoint compliance policy
Policy scope across devices and locations
Quarterly endpoint compliance policy review

capabilities.name: Asset hardening and compliance alignment
capabilities.description: Formal hardening processes align applications, endpoints, and network assets with compliance—including automation, patching, and quarterly review.
evaluation_focus: Application hardening process and coverage across SDLC
Endpoint and device hardening aligned to compliance
Network asset hardening process and automation
Quarterly review of hardening processes

capabilities.name: SOC compliance change monitoring
capabilities.description: Compliance changes are formally documented, securely transported, and dynamically captured for the SOC, including anomalous compliance drift detection.
evaluation_focus: Documented process for transporting compliance changes to the SOC
Secure transport of compliance changes to the SOC
Dynamic compliance changes captured and transferred to the SOC
Anomalous compliance drift or bulk exemption detection

capabilities.name: AI-era compliance governance
capabilities.description: Compliance management addresses AI system inventory, EU AI Act conformity obligations, and monitoring of AI vendor and model compliance posture.
evaluation_focus: AI system inventory and risk classification for compliance
EU AI Act conformity and documentation obligations tracked
AI vendor and third-party model compliance monitoring

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
